skill.name: sql-query-builder
skill.description: Build SQL queries from natural language and review execution plans with optimization guidance.
skill.summary: Convert database requests into SQL with plan review and tuning advice.
skill.author: Hermes-Sedimentary

security_audit.summary: The static weak cryptography finding at SKILL.md line 4 is a false positive because the line is a natural-language description and contains no cryptographic algorithm. The entropy finding at SKILL.md line 1 is also a false positive because the file is plain Markdown metadata and documentation, with no binary, encoded payload, prompt injection, scripts, network access, or filesystem behavior found.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: False positive. SKILL.md line 4 is a Chinese description of SQL query generation, execution plan analysis, and optimization advice. It does not mention or implement DES, MD5, SHA1, RC4, or any other weak cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact line contains only skill description metadata, not code or cryptographic configuration. No supporting evidence of cryptographic behavior appears elsewhere in the only scanned file.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: High File Entropy
low_findings.description: False positive. SKILL.md is readable Markdown with frontmatter, Chinese text, and short bullet lists. No encrypted blob, binary payload, base64 block, or obfuscated content was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows the file begins with normal YAML frontmatter and remains human-readable throughout. The entropy score is likely influenced by non-ASCII Chinese characters and punctuation rather than obfuscation.

content.user_title: Build SQL Queries From Plain Language
content.value_statement: Writing correct SQL from a business request can be slow and error-prone. This skill helps convert requests into SQL, review execution plans, and suggest query improvements.
actual_capabilities: Turns plain-language database requests into SQL query drafts.
Helps explain SQL query structure and expected behavior.
Reviews execution plan details supplied by the user.
Suggests query optimization approaches based on provided context.
Supports database and data analysis workflows across Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
limitations: Does not connect to databases or execute SQL by itself.
Requires schema details to produce accurate table and column references.
Generated SQL should be reviewed before use in production.
Optimization advice depends on the execution plan and database engine details provided.

use_cases.title: Draft Reporting Queries
use_cases.description: Convert a business question into a SELECT query with filters, joins, grouping, and ordering.

use_cases.title: Review Slow Queries
use_cases.description: Use a supplied execution plan to identify likely bottlenecks and practical tuning options.

use_cases.title: Translate Requirements
use_cases.description: Turn stakeholder requests into SQL drafts that engineers can review and refine.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Query
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with table and column names.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a SQL query for this request: [goal]. Tables: [tables]. Columns: [columns]. Use clear aliases and explain the result.

prompt_templates.title: Add Joins and Filters
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a SQL query that joins [tables] to answer [question]. Include filters for [conditions] and explain each join choice.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze an Execution Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Performance review for an existing query.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this SQL query and execution plan. Identify likely performance issues, explain the cause, and suggest safe optimization options.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize for Production Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced tuning with schema and index context.
prompt_templates.prompt: Optimize this query for [database engine]. Schema: [schema]. Indexes: [indexes]. Row counts: [counts]. Explain tradeoffs and validation steps.

output_examples.input: Find monthly revenue by customer segment for the last year.
output_examples.output: A structured SQL draft with date filtering, grouping by month and segment, revenue aggregation, and a short explanation of the result set.

output_examples.input: Review a slow order lookup query with an execution plan.
output_examples.output: A plain-language analysis of likely scan, join, or index issues, followed by tuning options and validation checks.

output_examples.input: Turn a product metric request into a query plan.
output_examples.output: A query outline that identifies needed tables, join keys, filters, metrics, and assumptions to confirm before implementation.

best_practices: Provide table names, column names, relationships, and the target database engine.
Share representative row counts and indexes when asking for optimization advice.
Review generated SQL with a qualified engineer before production use.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for accurate SQL without sharing the database schema.
Do not execute generated write queries against production without review.
Do not treat generic optimization advice as a replacement for measured database testing.

faq.question: Can this skill execute SQL queries?
faq.answer: No. It helps draft and review SQL, but it does not connect to a database or run queries.

faq.question: Which databases does it support?
faq.answer: It can help with common SQL patterns when you specify the target database engine and relevant syntax requirements.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the goal, table names, columns, relationships, filters, and any existing query or execution plan.

faq.question: Can it optimize slow queries?
faq.answer: Yes, when you provide the query, execution plan, indexes, and basic table size information.

faq.question: Is generated SQL production-ready?
faq.answer: No. Generated SQL should be tested, reviewed, and adjusted for your database engine before production use.

faq.question: Does it replace a database administrator?
faq.answer: No. It supports query drafting and review, but complex tuning still needs expert validation.
